_about: Curated per-platform follow-lists that 1B monitors. 'targets[]' is the normalized superset 1B/1C read; 'platform_fields' documents the original columns per platform so you can import/export your Notion tables faithfully. cherry_category uses category_enum.
_normalized_target_fields: Every targets[] row uses this superset; unused fields are null. platform-specific columns map into these names: handle/url -> source_url, 'Why I follow' -> why_follow, 'Top Audience' -> top_audience, 'Cherry Category'/'Category' -> cherry_category, 'Reviewer Notes' -> reviewer_notes, 'Created By' -> created_by, 'Targets' -> targets, 'Follow' -> follow.
_tier_fields: tier ('evergreen' default, or 'news') and check_frequency_days (per-row override; null means use the global REFRESH_DAYS) route a row to the right cadence: 1B/1D crawl 'evergreen' rows on the normal REFRESH_DAYS cycle; 1F News Monitor crawls 'news' rows on a short freshness window instead — see SEEDING_STRATEGY.md. 1B skips tier:'news' rows so they're never double-crawled.

targets.name: Example Person
targets.why_follow: posts concrete production AX rollouts with numbers
